claims.claim: Mean-variance portfolio optimization is a foundational concept in portfolio management and algorithmic trading.
claims.evidence_snippet: The seminal paper [23] by Markowitz in 1952 laid on the foundations of what is now popularly referred to as mean-variance portfolio optimization and modern portfolio theory.
claims.citation: Mean-Reverting Portfolio Design with Budget Constr, §Header/Preamble
claims.notes: Direct reference to Markowitz's seminal paper

claims.claim: Mean-variance portfolio design aims to find a tradeoff between expected return and risk measured by variance.
claims.evidence_snippet: The traditional mean-variance portfolio design problem is aimed at finding a tradeoff between the expected return and the risk measured by the variance.
claims.citation: Mean-Reverting Portfolio Design with Budget Constr, §Header/Preamble
claims.notes: Clear definition of mean-variance portfolio design

claims.claim: Random matrix theory (RMT) can be applied to estimate the covariance matrix in portfolio optimization.
claims.evidence_snippet: We analyze the effect of applying high-dimensionality estimators of the covariance matrix under the two discussed portfolio optimization strategies.
claims.citation: Random matrix theory and nested clustered portfoli, §Introduction
claims.notes: RMT is mentioned as a tool for estimating covariance matrix

claims.claim: The Markowitz mean-variance approach is a common method for building portfolios.
claims.evidence_snippet: The portfolios are built using the Markowitz mean-variance approach and our allocation variation of NCO.
claims.citation: Random matrix theory and nested clustered portfoli, §Introduction
claims.notes: Direct reference to Markowitz mean-variance approach

claims.claim: Shrinkage estimators can be used to estimate the mean-variance portfolio in high-dimensional cases.
claims.evidence_snippet: We construct a linear shrinkage estimator which is distribution-free and is optimal in the sense of maximizing with probability 1 the asymptotic out-of-sample expected utility.
claims.citation: Optimal shrinkage-based portfolio selection in hig, §Abstract
claims.notes: Shrinkage estimators are proposed as a solution for high-dimensional cases

claims.claim: Mean-variance portfolio optimization is related to classical methods in statistical signal processing.
claims.evidence_snippet: There are clear links between the subject of the paper and classical methods in statistical signal processing.
claims.citation: Optimal shrinkage-based portfolio selection in hig, §Introduction
claims.notes: Mean-variance optimization is mentioned in the context of signal processing

claims.claim: Markowitz's seminal paper (1952) suggests minimizing portfolio variance to determine optimal portfolio composition.
claims.evidence_snippet: The author suggests to determine the optimal composition of a portfolio of financial assets by minimizing the portfolio variance assuming that the expected portfolio return attains some prespecified fixed value.
claims.citation: Optimal shrinkage-based portfolio selection in hig, §Introduction
claims.notes: Direct reference to Markowitz's seminal paper

claims.claim: Mean-variance optimization justifies the advantages of diversification in asset management.
claims.evidence_snippet: This approach justifies the advantages of diversification and is a standard technique and benchmark in asset management.
claims.citation: Optimal shrinkage-based portfolio selection in hig, §Introduction
claims.notes: Mean-variance optimization is mentioned as a justification for diversification

claims.claim: The efficient frontier in the mean-standard deviation space can be obtained by varying the expected portfolio return.
claims.evidence_snippet: By varying this value we obtain the whole efficient frontier in the mean-standard deviation space.
claims.citation: Optimal shrinkage-based portfolio selection in hig, §Introduction
claims.notes: Clear explanation of how to obtain the efficient frontier
